title: Weekly Vessel Valuations Report, February 03 2026
summary:
Main
in Weekly Vessel Valuations Report 03/02/2026
Image reference: 2026-02-03_weekly-vessel-valuations-report-february-03-2026_img1.png
Bulkers: The market showed continued appetite for modern tonnage, with Greek and Chinese buyersbeing particularly active
Capesize Ally (185,900 DWT, Apr 2005, Kawasaki) and Mikata (177,200 DWT, Jun 2005, Namura) sold by Kian Gemi Yonetimi in an enbloc deal for USD 37 mil, VV Value USD 38.3 mil
Panamax KT Birdie (74,900 DWT, Mar 2011, Sasebo) sold SS/DD Passed to Greek buyers for USD 16.5 mil, VV Value USD 15.5 mil
Ultramax Mitsos (63,800 DWT, Jun 2013, Chengxi Shipyard) sold to Greek buyers for USD 20.5 mil, VV Value USD 18.9 mil
Ultramax The Loving (58,700 DWT, Jan 2007, Tsuneishi Cebu) sold SS/DD Passed to Chinese buyers for USD 11.9 mil, VV Value USD 11.1 mil
Tankers: VV values for mid-age and older product Tankers have firmed this week following an influx of secondhand deals. The VLCC S&P market stays active with another recorded Sinokor deal
VLCC Agneta Pallas III (319,100 DWT, Aug 2013, Hyundai Heavy Ind Ulsan Shipyard) sold to Sinokor for USD 90 mil, VV Value USD 92.6 mil
VLCC DHT Bauhinia (301,000 DWT, Jan 2007, Daewoo) sold by DHT Holding
